summary: MDM4U two-variable statistics problems fit a least-squares regression line ŷ = a + bx to paired data and measure the fit with the correlation coefficient r (between −1 and +1).
formulas: b = Sxy / Sxx, a = ȳ − b·x̄
method: Find the means x̄ and ȳ.
Compute the deviations and the sums Sxx = Σ(x−x̄)², Syy = Σ(y−ȳ)², Sxy = Σ(x−x̄)(y−ȳ).
Slope b = Sxy/Sxx; intercept a = ȳ − b·x̄ — that gives the line ŷ = a + bx.
Correlation r = Sxy/√(Sxx·Syy) measures the strength and direction of the linear fit.
Use the line only to predict within the range of the data.

example.title: Regression line and correlation
example.find: the least-squares line and the correlation r
